reviews.review_description: No lengthy tutorial for this one, just get on and go! Very simple instructions to get started and nothing too tricky to begin enjoying the awesome music and fun game play right away.  I started on easy mode and danced my way through the first set of songs.  There are multiple levels and difficulties with a lot of music to keep you from getting bored. I plan to work my way through them all on easy mode first, then notch it up.  I did the same thing in Audiotrip and loved it.  This is a little different because you use button controls in a rhythm game, instead of just swinging your arms to hit targets, but it's still fun and I don't think confusing at all, at least in easy mode!  So far, I've only had to hit the A button, left target button, and joystick left or right, matching onscreen prompts with the music, while I dance along to the beat for fun.  I love it and highly recommend you try this out if you like relaxing musical games.

reviews.review_title: Visual Button Prompts Have Lousy Info
reviews.review_description:
Tested on a Quest 3
The game is great, no issues running and looks nice visually with its retrosynth sort of style, decent enough music selection, gets more frantic on the harder difficult levels, but there is one thing that completely baffles me that prevents me from enjoying it.

On Flat Screen versions of the game, the button prompts are shaped like arrows and color coded so you can play the game without looking at the controller.  So with an Xbox controller, X is left and blue, A is down and green, B is red and right, exactly where the buttons are on the controller.

So what do they do with the Quest version?  The letters of the buttons are color coded so it matches the virtual overlay on the buttons (which is partly blocked largely by the virtual quest 2 halo bar, great design choice), but the directions for the buttons are incorrect making me have to keep on second guessing myself with every press, which is something you shouldn't be doing with a rhythm game.  

Instead of the prompts also being arrows, you have a gray dot around the button prompts, which are, A left side upper position which makes me think the upper button the left is the one to be pressed, but that's wrong, that's the Y button.  Then X button is right side upper position which logically falls to the upper button on the right controller, but nope, that is actually the B button. And finally, the B prompt is right side lower position, so lower button on the right controller, wrong, that is actually the A button.

Whoever programed the visual location of these prompts has made the game completely useless for me.  If you can get over this issue, or if you have the letter buttons as muscle memory in your mind, you have a fun corridor rhythm pressing game where you press the buttons as they appear in sync with the beat,  which you can get lost with if you really vibe with the song;  But one more warning, you might need some decent vr legs for when the stage rotates around in some sections.
I love this game a lot. I hope to see more like this one. It’s easy and fun, I hope everyone else enjoys it as much as I am.

reviews.review_title: Not an ideal way to play
reviews.review_description:
First off,  let me say that the music is the draw to this game. The controls for the VR port leave so much to be desired though.  Playing this game with the buttons being split between two controllers, both with thumb sticks, triggers and 2 buttons each really sucks. So much so,  that out borderline ruins the ENTIRE experience. It's not even like the game gains anything from using the VR controllers one bit. The Quest 2 and 3 are already capable of using controllers through BT so why not implement them as optional configurations?

Please fix this.
The game glitches like a lot for me, and it crashes the game and worst of all, my headset crashes, so I had to restart my headset and it was gone but don't buy this game.

reviews.review_title: As a Music - briliant as a game poor
reviews.review_description:
Well first of all as a music is the main thing in this game i would like to just hear that music some kind of jukebox will be appreciated.

As a game experience its not comfort thing. its like a guitar hero without guitar. Also game feels dizzy all people which i know (and they are VR players) so something wrong is with comfortable vr settings.

Amazing game for 1 euro. But I wouldn’t pay much more than that as the game has issues with frame rate and some other bugs that can be quite annoying. Also VR is totally an option here, this could be played on any other platform.
But again, for 1 euro, it was an amazing deal.

Best £1 I've spent. This is a relaxing rhythm game where you mostly press LT, a and b - and sometimes the direction button. The pacing feels right - I'm usually terrible with rhythm games but found this one easy and enjoyable like it's something to do while relaxing and watching the visuals - really makes you pay attention to the music. There's pauses where you go through a portal and just fly and don't need to press anything and just guide the ship through some rings and listen to the music. Using Quest Games Optimiser you can bump up the resolution and it's smooth like butter on the quest 3. After each level there's a 2d cutscene. Each 'world' has 4 songs you can complete in any order to unlock the next world. I've completed 2 worlds so far, need to get to world 4 to hear my favourite song 'levels' by avicii so I'm motivated to keep playing. You can play this just sitting down, no exercising required. I wish they'd update the controllers to be the quest 3 ones instead of quest 2 controllers - not that you ever look at them - but the rings on the mesh are unnecessary and make it harder to see the buttons. I don't get motion sickness from it - if you can handle rollercoasters in vr you can handle this just fine - plus the camera follows your ship and looking at a stationary object helps your brain with motion sickness.
You can play it either seating standing or lying down ,it’s very fun and relaxing, a lot of songs ￼ and comfortable to play￼, ￼￼￼I really recommend.
A celebration of the musical artist who left behind something beautiful. This simple game allows you to tap along with his songs. Soaring thru beautiful graphics....fly forever Avicci. "Tack".….

This game would be awesome, but the confusing meta quest touch controllers makes it almost impossible to play, there's a huge learning curve that if you're willing to learn, could make this experience enjoyable.

I think the devs just abandoned the game though so I'll not have my hopes high...

It's good for a dollar, but I'm glad I didn't buy this without the discount, I would have returned it.
If you love rhythm games and avicii, you can't go wrong here at $0.99. I am a very experienced rhythm gamer who found hard mode to be something I could dive right into and enjoy.

Saw this on sale, never heard of the guy but for a couple of bucks it seemed like a safe bet. The game was fun, the music was good and I found myself grooving. A half hour later I'm downloading his music for the first time. Wow, what an eye opener, 5 Stars!

reviews.review_title: Not bad. Not bad at all.
reviews.review_description: One of the best things about VR is the interactivity. It feels amazing to swing swords, climb cliffs, etc. There is a little downside though. There's something to be said for being able to sit back, kick up your feet and unwind with chill game. The fact that this was all button prompts kinda turned me off at first. VR rhythm games usually involve a lot of rhythmic flailing(you might call it dancing, but when I do it, trust me, it's flailing 😂) but I found myself really enjoying this, despite the lack of movement. Anyone who understands and enjoys a good flow state, don't sleep on this sale. This might be the best dollar I've ever spent.
What’s the sense of using VR here?
It’s amazing how have I not seen this game until now
And! This Game is Awesome! I finally got to experience AVICII in Real Life!
This game is so much fun and the music is great.  It gets better with each level. So glad I bought this one.

I liked it much more than expected. There are some songs that are very relaxing, others are danceable, some you can play calmly, others require a greater challenge. I know little about the artist and I know that they turned the game project into a tribute, but as I like musical games I decided to check it out and I didn't regret it.

reviews.review_title: love Avicii, but this is a miss
reviews.review_description: I love aviciis music, and I love this style of rhythm game, but this is a definite miss for me. it's nauseating in ways that other rhythm games aren't, the controls aren't very fun to use, and the music is incredibly limited. I'm glad this game was made in his memory, but I do not think VR is the right platform for it

reviews.review_title: Great rhythm game, ok VR game.
reviews.review_description:
Let's get the cons out of the way first. They did the bare minimum to port this into VR.
No motion controls are used in the levels, you can't play from the view of the cockpit for best immersion, and cutscenes are flat screens. Doesn't help that the cutscenes can't be replayed without resetting your progress (scores and all).

But this was already a great rhythm game, with lots of love towards Avicii, his impact on us all, and sending a message to be aware of what someone is going through, like what Avicii was going through.
So getting to play this game in 3D VR is kinda awesome. It's hard to soak in the environment around you as you soar through it, when you're focusing on the inputs, but there are still times you get wowed, especially during free-flying sections and the song menu.

It is a shame about the possibilities they missed with VR, like I mentioned before. And it doesn't help that some inputs have way too similar of colors for fast reactions.
But the game was already so good, that it's hard to not recommend on sale.

"AVICII Invector: Encore Edition" is a total blast, perfect for anyone who digs AVICII's tunes or just loves a good rhythm game. This version is packed with more tracks and cool features, making it a killer pick for both die-hard fans and newbies.

The gameplay is slick and intuitive, letting you jam through awesome abstract worlds perfectly synced to AVICII’s hits. It’s fun, challenging, and super engaging, whether you’re a casual player or a rhythm game junkie.

Plus, it’s a sweet tribute to AVICII, letting us all vibe with his legacy in the best way possible. Check it out and snag some great deals on my referral link at sheva[dot]bio[dot]link. If you're into thumping beats and sick visuals, this game’s definitely worth your time.

Excellent music and graphics, and turning this into a VR game was a no-braining - but the way it twists and turns just doesn’t translate well to VR. I found sitting down helps to keep things a little more stable, but after a few of those joystick turns and flying through the rings, I could feel my eyes spinning out of my head. Highly recommend this game on other flat screen consoles, but skip this if you’d be bothered by getting dizzy!

This game is excellent,I bought it 1st on Xbox (need to play with headphones) then I saw this on quest no question I had to double dip ,this was meant for VR the tunes the gameplay this is a gem of a game ,the only neg I can say is the a and b inputs need to be completely different colours because as is they are too similar,but don't let that put you off you will have a euphoric time ,one of my top 5 in my library

I have this game on Xbox one and absolutely love it. Unfortunately the control configuration for vr has made it very frustrating. Going from console with all abxy on the right side to this, where those buttons are split between left and right is difficult but doable. What bothers me more is that no motion controls were used. For any movement other than abxy, motion sensors should have been used, making the game not only more immersive but more user friendly and fun. Having to use a thumb stick means you're constantly needing to either move your thumb from joystick to a button on the same controller lightning fast or means you have to keep switching up between using left and right joystick which adds even more confusion to the button configuration. A quick upwards, left, or right movement with the controllers should have been implemented to achieve jumps and left/right rotation. Still recommended but I'm just very disappointed with the controls and will never achieve the scores and level progression I did on console. The game could have and deserved to be planned out better.

I really enjoy the flatscreen version of the game on my PC. Unfortunately, the VR version does not support playing with a Bluetooth controller, it is very awkward to play with the oculus touch controllers. Is proper controller support planed for the future?

With the default controller inputs, the game is barely 3*

I Hadn't spent much time with Tim's music before this game since I had a young child at his popularity peak, but it's a great way to get exposed to it if you aren't already a fan. Most consistent soundtrack of any music game, pretty much every track is a banger. 
It's basically a rhythm game with some fzero elements thrown in, with beautiful environments and effects, and a killer soundtrack. Knowing his story, it almost feels like he still lives on through this game, his little starship forever searching for happiness.

The difficulty ramps up as you go down the list of each difficulty mode, so playing the songs in the order listed rewards you with a slow increase in difficulty. 

The only negative thing about this game is there will never be more songs or a sequel. RIP Tim. Thanks for sharing your music with the world.

I have long loved Tim's music and Avicii Invector on the Ps4, so when I heard this was coming out on the Oculus Quest 2 I got really excited. That might not sound huge, but as someone who suffers from Major Depressive Disorder and Anhedonia, trust me, it really is.

I'm only about 35 minutes into playing it on the new platform, but overall I'm hugely impressed. I've only ever had issues with the free flying portions of the game, and either the new medium or your coding magicians have managed to smooth those choppy parts over.

I like that the movies were kept in that comic style, although I'm sure some would complain. And I'm sure some would complain of price, without knowing that a decent chunk goes to the Tim Bergling Foundation, which was set up (originally) to help tackle issues like mental health and suicide awareness and prevention, and has gone on to tackle additional issues like climate change and nature conservation.

I doubt I will find much wrong with this game in the near future, and look forward to thoroughly dissecting this game.

Thank you for the smile.

It was okayish… the tutorial was very unclear though. And after 2 minutes I was motion sick. Been playing VR for over a year… this was a disappointment after reading all the positive reviews… anyways I’ll get it refunded. I was not about to play more than 3 min :( got very dizzy

I love avicii and his music and was pretty pumped to find a rhythm game using his music but wasn’t a big fan of the controls. I didn’t get a VR set to sit on the couch and push buttons. It the game controlled like synth rider or beat saber it would have gotta 5 stars and then some but over all the controls just put me off.

This game would be so much better if they didn't have buttons to get the notes. Really takes away from the game and it doesn't feel as immersive as it could be. Disappointed. Other than that, gave 3 stars for the Avicii music and visuals (little spaceship)
The music are so great , it s  challenging  rythmic games even on easy mode ,i recommand this game for fox who likes spiritual music good vibes and real lyrics

Avicii's music fondly reminds me of the partying days that are behind behind me. Playing through this game is a lot of fun, and it's challenging enough that you're not just playing to listen to the songs.
First time playing this game because I’m a fan of Avicii’s music. It brings wonderful effect! Love it!

reviews.review_title: Devs - please make a free run mode!!
reviews.review_description:
The game is great - looks nice and is great to play!

However what caught me is being immersed to lovely looking graphics with a spacecraft while listening to Tim’s songs - an amazing feeling!

Thus please:
- add all released Avicii songs
- add a free to fly option to all music with beat matched gold lines and starry caves and fly through hoops and stuff

So that one can just enjoy the great feeling w/o being challenged at all.

That will make the game a 5 star and a daily source of happiness!
Cheers

I absolutely love music and rhythm games. This one is quite different in terms of VR games as it's from a third person perspective behind the ship. The music is really good and I've only done three levels so far. I would love to see a multiplayer feature added to this for up to three or four people To see who can get the best score on a course. I would also like to see integration of bhaptics vest and arms. I feel it would up the immersion and add to the wow factor that this game holds already. If you're into any sort of rhythm games and you like music, then you'll love this game! Don't sleep on it! Also the integration of using a game pad like any bluetooth controller would be an excellent touch! Happy Questing, y'all!

reviews.review_title: A great game, MUST BUY on sale.
reviews.review_description:
A VR third-person rhythm game based on the popular songs from Avicii, a DJ who passed away in 2018. As you could imagine, the decisions made to release the game afterward was difficult, resulting in all music net proceeds going directly to the Tim Bergling Foundation, which advocates for the recognition of a global health emergency and Safe In Our World, a games mental health charity. 

Good causes aside, the game plays in strikingly familiar fashion to games such as Audiosurf or Kingdom Hearts: Melody of Memory. You control a ship and must make controller inputs to the beat of the song. In some cases, you fly through rings using the joystick only. Fans of more "true to" rhythm games such as Guitar Hero or Rock Band might find difficulty in perfecting courses as you are not recreating the songs but playing a beat that compliments the original song. It adds a little variety to the songs but will require more direct memorisation of the course instead of completing the charts as close to the original as possible. 

The game is unique to VR through immersion of the various biome-themed levels, but the game is clearly intended for players with some VR acclimation as the speed of travel as well as the ship movement can induce nausea at first. The game itself is not very complex and could benefit from a higher framerate than available on the Meta Quest 3 (standalone). As a less active (and therefore sweaty and tiring) version of other rhythm-based games in VR, AVICII Invector is an absolute must buy when found on steep discount (which is common) and worth giving a try on sale. At full price, I would recommend purchasing it as a mega fan of Avicii or the genre he created music in. The game has a good foundation but leaves players wanting more once the barebones story mode is completed.
Just kinda boring. Was hoping for some cool visuals while listening to the music but it's just boring. The rhythm game is just not that fun to do in my opinion.

reviews.review_title: More of a cash grab than a tribute.
reviews.review_description: I had never heard Avicii's music, but I figured for $1 I couldn't go wrong.  I've enjoyed the music, but it's not something I'd have listened to otherwise.  This review is more about the game itself.  The gameplay couldn't be more basic, but the visuals are enjoyable.  There was obviously NO EFFORT put into converting this game to VR from whatever system it was originally developed for; the jarring, flat cutscenes make this abundantly clear.  If you're interested in hearing the music and see this game for $1, go for it; any more than that, pass.

Loved playing this on Switch. Got S+ on nearly every song on hard but the controls here are bit awkward and I'm sometimes struggling to get B ranks on hard while medium is too easy. Using both hands for the face buttons is definitely challenging though some may like how that makes the game feel new. I'm getting better with it but would prefer the ability to use a different controller to have all face buttons on the right hand and left hand for movement and beats.
Also the movement is controlled with either stick which makes the ship unintentionally move around when I barely touch the right stick as I'm hitting the A and B buttons.
Add additional controller support or a way to fix some of the traditional controls and it'll be perfect! Love seeing and hearing the game in a whole new way!
